IXACTSoundBank::Play

Plays a sound cue.

HRESULT Play(
    DWORD dwSoundCueIndex,
    PXACTSOUNDSOURCE pSoundSource,
    DWORD dwFlags,
    PXACTSOUNDCUE *ppSoundCue
);

Parameters

dwSoundCueIndex
[in] Sound cue index to play. When passing in a sound cue that has already been prepared, this parameter is not required and can be set to XACT_SOUNDCUE_INDEX_UNUSED (that is, it is not neccessary to track the sound cue index after calling IXACTSoundBank::Prepare).
pSoundSource
[in] A pointer to an IXACTSOUNDSOURCE on which to play the sound cue. This parameter can be NULL if the default sound source is desired.

Note    The default sound source (NULL) should be used for playing back any cue whose sounds play waves that are two or more channels (for example, stereo, 4.0, 5.1), as the custom 2D and 3D sound sources each use a MIXIN buffer (and thus can only play mono).

dwFlags
[in] Flags affecting playback. Can be zero or the following:
Value Description
XACT_FLAG_SOUNDCUE_AUTORELEASE Causes the cue to be automatically released when playback is finished (no sound cue pointer is returned). This has no effect on subsequent playback of the cue.
XACT_FLAG_SOUNDCUE_PREPARED This flag must be set when passing a valid sound cue instance from a prior call to the IXACTSoundBank::Prepare method.

This flag is not valid for use with the XACT_FLAG_SOUNDCUE_USE_LAST_VARIATION flag.
XACT_FLAG_SOUNDCUE_USE_LAST_VARIATION Indicates that the sound cue should use the last sound selected, assuming that multiple sounds are associated with the cue. That sound will continue to be selected until the function is called without specifying XACT_FLAG_SOUNDCUE_USE_LAST_VARIATION, at which point the sounds are selected at random again.

This does not affect wave variations. If the sound has a play event with wave variations, the waves will be selected at random. Similarly, pitch and volume variations are unaffected by this flag.

If no previous sound has been selected before using this flag, a sound will be selected at random.

This flag is not valid for use with the XACT_FLAG_SOUNDCUE_PREPARED flag.

Note    When calling Play with dwFlags set to zero, and passing a valid ppSoundCue out parameter, Play will fill ppSoundCue with a cue instance. However, if the cue holds a WMAPlayList then Play will return without setting ppSoundCue.

ppSoundCue
[in, out] The address of an IXACTSoundCue pointer. If the XACT_FLAG_SOUNDCUE_PREPARED flag is specified, this parameter must be a valid pointer (in parameter) to a sound cue obtained by calling the IXACTSoundBank::Prepare function. If this parameter is NULL, meaning no sound cue is desired, the XACT_FLAG_SOUNDCUE_AUTORELEASE flag must be specified. Otherwise, a sound cue pointer is returned.

Return Values

Returns DS_OK if successful, an error code otherwise.

Remarks

The layer of a sound cue is not applied until the cue is played. Calling Play with XACT_FLAG_SOUNDCUE_PRIME or calling IXACTSoundBank::Prepare will not interrupt playing cues.

Titles must call Stop to release sound cue pointers returned by Play, PlayEx, PrepareEx, or Prepare. For sound cues that play to completion, call Stop when the cue's stop notification arrives. Sound cue pointers are not returned (and need not be released) when XACT_FLAG_SOUNDCUE_AUTORELEASE is specified.

Requirements

Header: Declared in XACT.h.

Import Library: Use XACTEng.lib.

See Also

IXACTSoundBank::PlayEx, IXACTSOUNDSOURCE, IXACTSoundBank::Stop
